en.extract:
The sooty shrikethrush (Colluricincla tenebrosa) is a species of bird in the family Pachycephalidae.
It is found in the New Guinea Highlands.
Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.


Taxonomy and systematics
The sooty shrikethrush was formerly known as Colluricincla umbrina (Reichenow, 1915), as the name Rectes tenebrosus Hartlaub & Finsch, 1868 (the morningbird) has priority over Pachycephala tenebrosa Rothschild, 1911 (the sooty shrikethrush) when the two species are placed in the same genus; with the transfer of the morningbird from the genus Colluricincla to Pachycephala in 2013, the sooty shrikethrush reverted from umbrina to the older name tenebrosa. Alternate names for the sooty shrikethrush include the obscure shrike-thrush, sooty robin-whistler and sooty whistler.


Subspecies
Two subspecies are recognized:

C. t. atra – (Rothschild, 1931): Found on northern slopes of central New Guinea mountains
C. t. tenebrosa – (Rothschild, 1911): Found on southern slopes of central New Guinea mountains


References


External links
Sooty Strikethrush at Birds of the World (Cornell Lab)

The '''sooty shrikethrush''' ('''''Colluricincla tenebrosa''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Pachycephalidae]].
It is found in the [[New Guinea Highlands]].
Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.

==Taxonomy and systematics==
The sooty shrikethrush was formerly known as ''Colluricincla umbrina'' <small>(Reichenow, 1915)</small>, as the name ''Rectes tenebrosus'' <small>Hartlaub & Finsch, 1868</small> (the [[morningbird]]) has [[Principle of Priority|priority]] over ''Pachycephala tenebrosa'' <small>Rothschild, 1911</small> (the sooty shrikethrush) when the two species are placed in the same genus; with the transfer of the morningbird from the genus ''Colluricincla'' to ''[[Pachycephala]]'' in 2013, the sooty shrikethrush reverted from ''umbrina'' to the older name ''tenebrosa''.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/archive-3-1-to-3-5/taxonomy-3-1-3-5/|title=Taxonomy 3.1-3.5 « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-02-13}}</ref> Alternate names for the sooty shrikethrush include the '''obscure shrike-thrush''', '''sooty robin-whistler''' and '''sooty whistler'''.

===Subspecies===
Two subspecies are recognized:<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|title=IOC World Bird List 6.3|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref|journal=IOC World Bird List Datasets|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.6.3|doi-access=free}}</ref>
* ''C. t. atra'' – <small>([[Walter Rothschild, 2nd Baron Rothschild|Rothschild]], 1931)</small>: Found on northern slopes of central [[New Guinea]] mountains
* ''C. t. tenebrosa'' – <small>(Rothschild, 1911)</small>: Found on southern slopes of central New Guinea mountains
